title: Brihaspati Chalisa
intro: Shri Brihaspati Chalisa: Full Path, Meaning, and Benefits - Hymn to Lord Brihaspati
description:
|| Shri Brihaspati Dev Chalisa (Guru Nikhileshwaranand Stuti) ||

|| Doha ||
Pranavau pratham guru charan, buddhi gyan gun khan.
Shri Ganesh sharad sahit, bason hraday mein aan.
Agyani mati mand main, hain guruswami sujan.
Doshon se main bhara hua hoon tum ho kripa nidhan.

|| Chaupai ||
Jai Narayan jai Nikhileshvar. Vishva prasiddh akhil tantreshvar.
Yantra-mantra vigyan ke gyata. Bharat bhu ke prem prenata.
Jab jab hui dharam ki hani. Siddhashram ne pathaye gyani.
Sachchidanand guru ke pyare. Siddhashram se aap padhare.
Uchchakoti ke rishi-muni svechha. Oye karan dharam ki raksha.
Abki baar aapki baari. Trahi trahi hai dhara pukari.
Marundhar prant kharantiya grama. Multanchand pita kar nama.
Sheshshahi sapne mein aaye. Mata ko darshan dikhlaye.
Rupadevi matu ati dharmik. Janam bhayo shubh ikkis tarikh.
Janam divas tithi shubh sadhak ki. Puja karte aradhak ki.
Janam vrutant sunaye navina. Mantra Narayan naam kari dina.
Naam Narayan bhav bhay hari. Siddh yogi manav tan dhari.
Rishivar brahma tatva se urjit. Aatma swarup guru goravanvit.
Ek baar sang sakha bhavan mein. Kari snaan lage chintan mein.
Chintan karat samadhi laagi. Sudh-budh heen bhaye anuragi.
Purn kari sansar ki reeti. Shankar jaise bane grihasthi.
Adbhut sangam prabhu maya ka. Avalokan hai vidhi chhaya ka.
Yug-yug se bhav bandhan reeti. Janha Narayan vahi bhagwati.
Sansarik man hue ati glani. Tab himgiri gaman ki thani.
Atharah varsh Himalaya ghume. Sarv siddhiaya guru pag chume.
Tyag atal Siddhashram aasan. Karam bhumi aaye Narayan.
Dhara gagan brahman mein gunji. Jai Gurudev sadhana punji.
Sarv dharmahit shivir purodha. Karmakshetra ke atulit yodha.
Hraday vishal shastra bhandara. Bharat ka bhautik ujiyara.
Ek sau chhappan granth rachayita. Sidhi sadhak vishva vijeta.
Priya lekhak priya gudh pravakta. Bhoot-bhavishya ke aap vidhata.
Ayurved jyotish ke sagar. Shodash kala yukt parameshwar.
Ratan parkhi vighan haranta. Sanyasi ananyatam santa.
Adbhut chamatkar dikhlaya. Parad ka shivling banaya.
Ved puran shastra sab gaate. Pareshwar durlabh kehlate.
Puja kar nit dhyan lagave. Vo nar Siddhashram mein jaave.
Charo ved kanth mein dhare. Pujaniya jan-jan ke pyare.
Chintan karat mantra jab gaayein. Vishwamitra Vashishtha bulayein.
Mantra namo Narayan sancha. Dhyanat bhagat bhoot-pishacha.
Pratah kal karahi nikhilayan. Man prasann nit tejasvi tan.
Nirmal man se jo bhi dhyave. Riddhi siddhi sukh-sampati paave.
Path karahi nit jo chalisa. Shanti pradan karahi yogisa.
Ashtottar shat path karat jo. Sarv siddhiaya pavat jan so.

|| Doha ||
Shri guru charan ki dhara. Siddhashram sadhak parivara.
Jai-jai-jai anand ke swami. Baram-bar namami namami.
meaning:
|| Shri Brihaspati Dev Chalisa (Guru Nikhileshwaranand Stuti) ||

|| Doha ||
Pranavau pratham guru charan, buddhi gyan gun khan.
(I bow first to the feet of the Guru, who is a mine of intellect, knowledge, and virtues.)
Shri Ganesh sharad sahit, bason hraday mein aan.
(Along with Shri Ganesh and Sharada (Saraswati), may you come and reside in my heart.)
Agyani mati mand main, hain guruswami sujan.
(I am ignorant and slow-witted, while you are the wise Guruswami.)
Doshon se main bhara hua hoon tum ho kripa nidhan.
(I am filled with faults, and you are the ocean of mercy.)

|| Chaupai ||
Jai Narayan jai Nikhileshvar. Vishva prasiddh akhil tantreshvar.
(Victory to Narayan, victory to Nikhileshwara. You are world-famous and the master of all Tantras.)
Yantra-mantra vigyan ke gyata. Bharat bhu ke prem prenata.
(You are the knower of the science of Yantra-Mantra and the inspiration of love for the land of Bharat.)
Jab jab hui dharam ki hani. Siddhashram ne pathaye gyani.
(Whenever religion has declined, Siddhashram has sent wise ones.)
Sachchidanand guru ke pyare. Siddhashram se aap padhare.
(O beloved of Sachchidanand Guru, you have arrived from Siddhashram.)
Uchchakoti ke rishi-muni svechha. Oye karan dharam ki raksha.
(Sages of high order come of their own will to protect religion.)
Abki baar aapki baari. Trahi trahi hai dhara pukari.
(This time it is your turn, for the Earth is crying out in distress.)
Marundhar prant kharantiya grama. Multanchand pita kar nama.
(In the Kharantiya village of the Marundhar region, your father's name is Multanchand.)
Sheshshahi sapne mein aaye. Mata ko darshan dikhlaye.
(Lord Sheshshahi came in a dream and gave a vision to your mother.)
Rupadevi matu ati dharmik. Janam bhayo shubh ikkis tarikh.
(Your mother Rupadevi was very religious. You were born on the auspicious 21st date.)
Janam divas tithi shubh sadhak ki. Puja karte aradhak ki.
(This day is auspicious for the practitioners; the devotees worship it.)
Janam vrutant sunaye navina. Mantra Narayan naam kari dina.
(You narrated the new account of birth and gave the Narayan Mantra.)
Naam Narayan bhav bhay hari. Siddh yogi manav tan dhari.
(The name Narayan dispels the fears of existence; you are a Siddha Yogi in human form.)
Rishivar brahma tatva se urjit. Aatma swarup guru goravanvit.
(O noble sage, you are energized by the Brahma element; you are the soul-form Guru, filled with glory.)
Ek baar sang sakha bhavan mein. Kari snaan lage chintan mein.
(Once with friends in the house, after bathing, you became absorbed in contemplation.)
Chintan karat samadhi laagi. Sudh-budh heen bhaye anuragi.
(While contemplating, you entered Samadhi, losing worldly consciousness in your devotion.)
Purn kari sansar ki reeti. Shankar jaise bane grihasthi.
(Fulfilling the ways of the world, you became a householder like Shankar (Lord Shiva).)
Adbhut sangam prabhu maya ka. Avalokan hai vidhi chhaya ka.
(This is a wonderful confluence of God's illusion; it is a vision of divine shadow.)
Yug-yug se bhav bandhan reeti. Janha Narayan vahi bhagwati.
(The tradition of worldly bonds has existed for ages; where there is Narayan, there is Bhagwati.)
Sansarik man hue ati glani. Tab himgiri gaman ki thani.
(When the worldly mind felt great disgust, then you decided to go to the Himalayas.)
Atharah varsh Himalaya ghume. Sarv siddhiaya guru pag chume.
(You wandered the Himalayas for eighteen years, where all Siddhis kissed the Guru's feet.)
Tyag atal Siddhashram aasan. Karam bhumi aaye Narayan.
(Leaving the firm seat of Siddhashram, Narayan came to the land of action.)
Dhara gagan brahman mein gunji. Jai Gurudev sadhana punji.
(The earth and sky echoed with "Jai Gurudev," the capital of Sadhana.)
Sarv dharmahit shivir purodha. Karmakshetra ke atulit yodha.
(You are the leader of the welfare camps for all religions and an incomparable warrior of the field of action.)
Hraday vishal shastra bhandara. Bharat ka bhautik ujiyara.
(You have a vast heart and are a treasury of scriptures; you are the material light of Bharat.)
Ek sau chhappan granth rachayita. Sidhi sadhak vishva vijeta.
(You are the author of 156 books; you are a world-conquering Siddha-sadhak.)
Priya lekhak priya gudh pravakta. Bhoot-bhavishya ke aap vidhata.
(You are a beloved writer and a profound speaker; you are the creator of the past and future.)
Ayurved jyotish ke sagar. Shodash kala yukt parameshwar.
(You are the ocean of Ayurveda and Astrology; you are the Supreme Lord endowed with sixteen arts.)
Ratan parkhi vighan haranta. Sanyasi ananyatam santa.
(You are the judge of gems and the remover of obstacles; you are an incomparable Sanyasi saint.)
Adbhut chamatkar dikhlaya. Parad ka shivling banaya.
(You showed a wonderful miracle and created a Shivling of mercury.)
Ved puran shastra sab gaate. Pareshwar durlabh kehlate.
(The Vedas, Puranas, and scriptures all sing of you; you are called the rare Lord of Mercury.)
Puja kar nit dhyan lagave. Vo nar Siddhashram mein jaave.
(One who worships and meditates daily reaches Siddhashram.)
Charo ved kanth mein dhare. Pujaniya jan-jan ke pyare.
(You hold all four Vedas in your throat; you are worshipable and beloved of all people.)
Chintan karat mantra jab gaayein. Vishwamitra Vashishtha bulayein.
(When you sing the mantra in contemplation, you summon Vishwamitra and Vashishtha.)
Mantra namo Narayan sancha. Dhyanat bhagat bhoot-pishacha.
(The mantra 'Namo Narayan' is true; by meditating on it, ghosts and demons flee.)
Pratah kal karahi nikhilayan. Man prasann nit tejasvi tan.
(By remembering you in the morning, the mind becomes happy and the body becomes radiant.)
Nirmal man se jo bhi dhyave. Riddhi siddhi sukh-sampati paave.
(Whoever meditates with a pure mind receives Riddhi-Siddhi (wealth and prosperity).)
Path karahi nit jo chalisa. Shanti pradan karahi yogisa.
(Whoever recites this Chalisa daily receives peace from the Yogi.)
Ashtottar shat path karat jo. Sarv siddhiaya pavat jan so.
(Whoever recites it 108 times attains all Siddhis.)

|| Doha ||
Shri guru charan ki dhara. Siddhashram sadhak parivara.
(The flow of the Guru's feet is the entire family of Siddhashram practitioners.)
Jai-jai-jai anand ke swami. Baram-bar namami namami.
(Victory to the Lord of Bliss; I bow to you again and again.)

faq.q1: Why is the Brihaspati Chalisa recited?
faq.a1: Brihaspati Dev is known as the 'Guru of Gods.' This Chalisa is recited to attain wisdom, intellect, discernment, and spiritual growth. It is highly effective for seeking proper guidance in life.
faq.q2: What is the most auspicious day to recite it?
faq.a2: Thursday (Guruvar) is dedicated to the worship of Brihaspati Dev. Therefore, reciting it on a Thursday is considered the most auspicious and rewarding.
faq.q3: Does the Brihaspati Chalisa help in removing obstacles to marriage?
faq.a3: Yes, according to astrology, Brihaspati is the significator of marriage. If there is a delay or obstacles in marriage, regular recitation of this Chalisa is considered very beneficial.
faq.q4: Is this Chalisa helpful in achieving success in career and education?
faq.a4: Absolutely. Brihaspati Dev is the lord of knowledge. For students and professionals, reciting this helps in increasing concentration and developing the ability to make the right decisions.
faq.q5: Is any special material required for this?
faq.a5: Brihaspati Dev favors yellow items. Wearing yellow clothes while reciting, offering yellow lentils (Chana Dal) or jaggery as 'Bhog,' and using yellow flowers are considered especially auspicious.
